title: End-Stage Kidney Disease (ESKD)
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: kidneys can no longer sustain life
needs dialysis or transplant to survive
nephrologist's clinical determination, not a number
end-stage kidney disease requiring kidney replacement therapy

title: Kidney Transplantation
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: graft placed in the recipient's pelvis
native kidneys usually left in place
renal artery anastomosed to common iliac artery
increased CMV, EBV, polyomavirus infection risk
living or deceased donor kidney transplant

title: Transplant Rejection
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: three-fold malignancy risk on chronic immunosuppression
recipient immune response against donor kidney

title: Hemodialysis
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: heparin given to prevent filter clotting
intradialytic hypotension from rapid fluid removal
urea, creatinine, potassium diffuse into dialysate

title: Arteriovenous (AV) Fistula Access
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: extremely low infection risk versus catheters
needs weeks to mature before use
thrombosis or stenosis is most common complication

title: Peritoneal Dialysis
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: peritoneal capillaries serve as the membrane
cloudy effluent with abdominal pain and fever
complicated by hernias and pleural effusions
Staph epidermidis bacterial peritonitis is most serious
intraperitoneal antibiotics via tunneled abdominal catheter

title: Continuous Renal Replacement Therapy (CRRT)
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: ICU setting with shock or hypotension
central venous catheter required for access
gradual fluid removal, minimal hemodynamic shifts
tolerated when intermittent hemodialysis is not
used in acute kidney injury with instability
continuous renal replacement therapy in critically ill

title: Dialysis-Related Amyloidosis
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: bilateral shoulder pain in dialysis patient
median nerve entrapment at the wrist
amyloid deposition in synovium and joints
protein not cleared by dialysis membranes
carpal tunnel syndrome in hemodialysis patient

title: AEIOU Indications for Acute Dialysis
brick_title: End-Stage Kidney Disease and Kidney Replacement Therapy
buzzwords: AEIOU mnemonic for acute dialysis indications
